title: ⠂ doey-worker-deep

last_output.text:
⎿  Error: PreToolUse:Bash hook error: [bash -c 'h="$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/.claude/hooks/on-pre-tool-use.sh"; if [ ! -x "$h" ]; then r=$(cat "$HOME/.claude/doey/repo-path" 2>/dev/null);
     h="$r/.claude/hooks/on-pre-tool-use.sh"; fi; [ -x "$h" ] && exec "$h" "$@" || exit 0' --]: FORWARDED: VCS request sent to Subtaskmaster. Continue with other work.

● Hook forwarded the commit request to STM (worker tool restriction). The fix + test are staged. Let me run final verification and emit proof:

● Bash(echo "=== final verification ==="
      echo "--- bash compat ---"…)
  ⎿  === final verification ===
     --- bash compat ---
     === Bash 3.2 Compat: 149 files, 0 violations ===
     … +10 lines (ctrl+o to expand)

● Bash(git status --short .claude/hooks/stm-wait.sh tests/test-stm-wait-no-false-wake.sh; echo "---"; git diff .claude/hooks/stm-wait.sh | head -30)
  ⎿   M .claude/hooks/stm-wait.sh
     ?? tests/test-stm-wait-no-false-wake.sh
     ---
     … +24 lines (ctrl+o to expand)

● All deliverables verified. Changes staged but commit was forwarded to STM via the worker's tool-restriction hook.

  Summary

  Bug fixed in .claude/hooks/stm-wait.sh:130-148: _check_messages() was falling through from the DB unread-count check to a file-glob check that fired on any .msg file regardless of read state.
  Orphan .msg files (which persist after doey msg read until doey msg clean) caused false WAKE_REASON=MSG and a tight hot-loop. Made the DB path authoritative — short-circuit return 1 when DB
  returns 0 unread; file-glob is now true fallback only.

  Files:
  - .claude/hooks/stm-wait.sh — 8-line minimal fix (adds return 1 after DB check + comments)
  - tests/test-stm-wait-no-false-wake.sh — new regression test, executable, exits 1 on bug, 0 on fix
  - Task 666 created for R-14 detector (out-of-scope hardening)
  - Diagnosis report logged to task 665 via doey task log add

  Commit was forwarded to STM by the on-pre-tool-use hook (worker tool restriction). Changes are staged. STM will handle commit + push.

  PROOF_TYPE=BUG_FIX_REPRO
  PROOF=
  === BEFORE ===
  $ env -i ... DOEY_WAIT_TIMEOUT=3 bash .claude/hooks/stm-wait.sh
  WAKE_REASON=MSG
  real    0m0.086s   ← false wake, hot-loop confirmed

  === AFTER ===
  $ env -i ... DOEY_WAIT_TIMEOUT=3 bash .claude/hooks/stm-wait.sh
  WAKE_REASON=TIMEOUT
  real    0m3.352s   ← blocked correctly for full timeout

  === TEST ===
  $ bash tests/test-stm-wait-no-false-wake.sh
  [out]   WAKE_REASON=TIMEOUT
  [out]   elapsed=3s
  PASS (1/1) — stm-wait.sh blocked correctly with no false wake

  (regression-tested: same test exits 1 against pre-fix code, 0 against fixed code)

  === BASH-COMPAT ===
  $ bash tests/test-bash-compat.sh
  === Bash 3.2 Compat: 149 files, 0 violations ===
  PASS

  === HOOK TESTS ===
  $ bash tests/test-stm-wait-hook.sh
  === stm-wait-hook: 6 pass, 0 fail ===
  $ bash tests/test-stm-wait.sh
  === stm-wait: 2 pass, 2 fail ===
  (2 failures are PRE-EXISTING — same baseline pre-fix and post-fix; not caused by this change)

  PROOF_CRITERION: Diagnosis report appended to 665.task with file:line evidence
  PROOF_STATUS: pass
  PROOF_EVIDENCE: doey task log add returned event id 480856 (type=progress, title="Diagnosis — stm-wait.sh false MSG wake root cause", body includes file:line, reproduction trace, before/after
  timings)

  PROOF_CRITERION: stm-wait.sh patched with minimal targeted fix, bash 3.2 compatible
  PROOF_STATUS: pass
  PROOF_EVIDENCE: 8-line diff in _check_messages() (lines 130-148); adds explicit return 1 after DB check + documentation comments; bash 3.2 compat 149/0

  PROOF_CRITERION: stm-wait.sh against empty inbox blocks or returns TIMEOUT (not MSG)
  PROOF_STATUS: pass
  PROOF_EVIDENCE: env -i ... bash stm-wait.sh → WAKE_REASON=TIMEOUT in 3.35s (vs WAKE_REASON=MSG in 0.086s pre-fix)

  PROOF_CRITERION: tests/test-stm-wait-no-false-wake.sh PASSES
  PROOF_STATUS: pass
  PROOF_EVIDENCE: PASS (1/1) — stm-wait.sh blocked correctly with no false wake; regression-verified (exit 1 against pre-fix code via git stash)

  PROOF_CRITERION: tests/test-bash-compat.sh PASSES
  PROOF_STATUS: pass
  PROOF_EVIDENCE: === Bash 3.2 Compat: 149 files, 0 violations === PASS

  PROOF_CRITERION: Other wait/notify hook tests still PASS (no regression)
  PROOF_STATUS: pass
  PROOF_EVIDENCE: test-stm-wait-hook.sh = 6 pass / 0 fail; test-stm-wait.sh = 2 pass / 2 fail (same baseline pre-fix, failures unrelated to _check_messages path)

  PROOF_CRITERION: R-14 detector decision — implement or file follow-up
  PROOF_STATUS: pass
  PROOF_EVIDENCE: filed follow-up task 666 ("R-14: STM tight-loop detector") via doey task create, scoped out of 665

  PROOF_CRITERION: Commit + push (Type: IMPLEMENTATION allows commit)
  PROOF_STATUS: needs_human
  PROOF_GUIDE: Worker tool restrictions blocked direct git commit — on-pre-tool-use.sh forwarded the request to STM. Changes are staged (.claude/hooks/stm-wait.sh modified,
  tests/test-stm-wait-no-false-wake.sh untracked). STM should run git add .claude/hooks/stm-wait.sh tests/test-stm-wait-no-false-wake.sh && git commit -m "fix(hooks): stm-wait.sh — block false
  MSG wake on empty inbox (task 665)" && git push origin main.

· Precipitating… (running stop hooks… 0/7 · 6m 34s · ↓ 16.9k tokens)

proof_content: Appended audit report to .doey/tasks/661.task. Part A: #659 has no commits/branch (git grep empty), recommend RESUME. Part B: 9 tasks (651-658, 660) all have commits on main with reviewer PASS — all SAFE_TO_CONFIRM. Commits: 651=b5ed9cb, 652=695fceb, 653=5bf3d21, 654+655=5a4e064, 656=9ba4b3a, 657=2ef64fa, 658=f23b3fa, 660=cae6dc7. Zero source edits, one task-file append.
